title: Setuphelfer
subtitle: Analyze first. Backup second. Act safely.
hint: Nothing is repaired or installed unless you explicitly confirm.
menuPrompt: What would you like to do?

backup.title: Backup & data safety
backup.subtitle: Full backup and quick file rescue to external targets.

analyze.title: Analysis
analyze.subtitle: System analysis — computer, status and recommendations.

partitions.title: Partition helper
partitions.subtitle: Understand drives and plan a safe layout.

linuxMigration.title: Switch this PC to Linux
linuxMigration.subtitle: Analyze Windows or existing systems, back up data, and plan Linux installation.

dataRescue.title: Rescue files
dataRescue.subtitle: Read important Windows folders and create a rescue plan.

analyze.intro: Overview of computer, services and storage — without a full partition table.
analyze.recWindowsFound: Windows data found — back up before making changes.
analyze.recBackupFirst: Back up data before making changes.
analyze.recTargetMissing: No external backup target — connect a USB drive.

backup.quickRescueHint: Read important Windows folders and copy to external target — without changing partitions.

partitions.intro: Partitions and drives will appear here once disk discovery is active.
partitions.introLoaded: Detected drives and partitions (read-only). Write actions only via backup with confirmation.
partitions.hint: Source and target drives are checked separately in the backup area.
partitions.helperIntro: Understand drives and plan a safe layout — nothing is changed here.
partitions.beginnerNote: Nothing is changed here. Setuphelfer only explains the current layout.
partitions.goalTitle: What would you like to do?

goals.external_backup: Use external disk as backup target

partitions.recSwap: Swap only if enabled — then fastest internal drive. USB for backup or tests only.

linuxInstall.intro: Reinstall is shown as plan-only preview on the rescue stick — no automatic formatting or install without explicit approval.
linuxInstall.statusBlocked: Execute blocked — plan-only in public rescue. Backup and analysis first.
linuxInstall.rescueHint: From rescue stick: backup and verify first, then partitioning via Setuphelfer desktop.
linuxInstall.wipeHint: To wipe data and install Linux: create a plan under “Switch this PC to Linux”, rescue files and verify backup first. The stick never formats disks automatically.

dataRescue.intro: Rescue important files from Windows before partitions change or Linux is installed. Nothing is deleted, formatted, or overwritten.
dataRescue.planOnlyBanner: Read and plan only — copy start is enabled in the next step (RS-P3V.1).
dataRescue.noWindowsHint: The Windows partition must be mounted read-only. The rescue stick tries this automatically. If it fails: disable Windows Fast Startup and reboot.
dataRescue.noNtfs: No NTFS partition detected (Windows drive not found).
dataRescue.usersPathMissing: Windows found but Users folder is missing or damaged — system may be severely corrupted.

diagnostics.mount_failed: Could not mount Windows read-only. Disable Fast Startup in Windows and reboot.
diagnostics.no_ntfs: No Windows NTFS partition found. Check if Windows is installed or the disk is encrypted.
diagnostics.users_empty_or_damaged: Users folder is empty or unreadable — profiles may be damaged or removed.
diagnostics.windows_without_users: Windows system found but no user profiles — reinstall or corruption possible.
diagnostics.ntfs_not_mounted: NTFS present but not mounted — automatic mount is attempted.
diagnostics.unknown: No Windows user profile found. Check all NTFS partitions.

dataRescue.confirmNoDelete: Copy only — nothing deleted, formatted, or overwritten.

linuxMigration.intro: This assistant guides you step by step to Linux. Nothing is deleted or installed — only analysis, recommendations, and an installation plan.
linuxMigration.planOnlyBanner: No changes are made to storage devices. This feature creates analysis and installation suggestions only.
linuxMigration.identityConfirm: This is the computer I want to switch to Linux.
linuxMigration.profileTitle: What will you use Linux for?
linuxMigration.variantsTitle: How do you want to switch?
linuxMigration.partitionsPreviewOnly: Preview only — nothing is partitioned or formatted.
linuxMigration.dualDiskIntro: Multiple internal drives detected — recommendation per Linux best practice:
linuxMigration.dualDiskSingle: One internal disk detected — system and data planned on it.
linuxMigration.warningBody: The selected migration may erase all data on this computer.
linuxMigration.warningContinue: You may continue — backup is strongly recommended but not mandatory.
linuxMigration.confirmDataLoss: I understand that data may be lost.
linuxMigration.confirmSafe: This option should not erase existing data. You can create the installation plan.

panda.welcome: Hi! I'll guide you to Linux — with no hidden actions.
panda.identity: Please verify this is really your computer.
panda.profile: Choose your main use case — the partition suggestion adapts to it.
panda.partitions: This is only a suggestion. Partitioning is planned later in the partition helper.
panda.readiness: Green = well prepared, yellow = review hints, red = resolve critical items.
panda.analysisDone: Analysis complete. Here is what is inside your computer.
panda.disks: Fast SSDs suit Linux and apps; large drives suit photos and backups.
panda.variants: Green = recommended, yellow = caution, red = data loss possible.
panda.distros: For beginners we recommend Linux Mint or Ubuntu LTS.
panda.backup: A backup protects your files — you may continue without one, but we strongly advise it.
panda.confirm: Read carefully. A plan is created only after confirmation — nothing is written to disk yet.
panda.planReady: This is only a plan. Nothing is installed or erased yet.

replace.title: Replace Windows completely
replace.con1: All existing data will be erased

newDisk.title: Use a new Linux system drive

linuxMint.hardware: Good for typical PCs and laptops with 4 GB RAM or more.

ubuntu.desc: Popular Linux with LTS support and lots of help online.
ubuntu.hardware: Runs on most PCs and laptops.
ubuntu.con1: Snap packages not loved by everyone

debian.desc: Very stable — good for experienced users.
debian.hardware: Efficient, runs on older hardware too.

debianServer.desc: Debian without desktop — for servers and services.

ubuntuServer.desc: Ubuntu Server LTS for cloud and home servers.
ubuntuServer.hardware: Good for x86 servers and VMs.

settings.telemetryTestSpooled: Spooled locally on stick (upload server unreachable).
settings.expertHint: Enable expert mode to view technical raw data.
settings.noSystemdDump: No systemd errors on the standard screen.

restore.title: Restore
restore.subtitle: Restore your system from a backup

cloudserver_manage.title: Manage cloud server
cloudserver_manage.subtitle: Analyze and secure your cloud server

network.telemetryLocalFail: Local telemetry health unreachable — check backend.
network.noAutoConnect: No connection without your explicit confirmation.
network.telemetryPeerHint: This machine is visible to the Setuphelfer health server.
network.radioOff: Wi-Fi radio off — enabling for scan

systemMenu.title: System
systemMenu.tuiHint: Text UI: GRUB entry “Text mode” or F10 → Reboot.
systemMenu.shutdownHint: Shutdown not available — use the device power button.

backup.guideStep1: Connect an external drive via USB (not the rescue stick).
backup.guideStep2: Under “Target drive”, pick the external disk — not /dev/sda (stick).
backup.guideStep3: Click “Mount ext4 …” if the backup partition is not mounted yet.
backup.guideStep4: Pick a source: for tests use a small USB NTFS disk or NTFS partition.
backup.guideStep5: Click “Create backup plan” — then “Start backup” when the plan is ready.
backup.guideNote: Leave encryption off for the first test. Mount failed? Replug the drive and try again.
backup.mountHint: The ext4 partition on the target disk must be mounted — otherwise no start button.

boot.title: Setuphelfer Rescue
boot.subtitle: Rescue assistant
boot.pleaseWait: Please wait — loading interface and services.

guide.title: Quick guide
guide.summary: 1) Rescue files  2) Backup  3) Plan Linux. Nothing changes without your confirmation.
guide.step1: Save important Windows folders under “Rescue files”.
guide.step2: Create a full backup on an external drive.
guide.step3: Create an installation plan under “Switch this PC to Linux”.
guide.step4: Reinstall only after plan and backup — the stick never formats automatically.
